title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. valesiaca
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. valesiaca, also known as mountain kidney-vetch, is a subspecies of the kidney-vetch plant, a flowering perennial found in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins. The medicinal properties of mountain kidney-vetch have been linked to several health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against infections. Herbal preparations of mountain kidney-vetch can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin conditions.
